name: Buffered activation ratio
biological_rationale: Disease states often amplify one module while suppressing or buffering another. A short ratio can expose that asymmetry.

name: Balance law
biological_rationale: A normalized contrast is a compact way to test whether disease reflects a shift in balance rather than a simple univariate increase.

name: Log contrast law
biological_rationale: Log contrasts can stabilize heavy-tailed module scores while preserving a short, interpretable law family.

name: Simple additive interaction
biological_rationale: A short difference law is a useful skeptic baseline because many disease effects reduce to a shift between opposing programs.

name: Single-feature anchor
biological_rationale: A one-feature law is the simplest interpretable baseline and must remain competitive in the final comparison.

name: HIF/angiogenesis vs normal kidney
biological_rationale: CA9 is a HIF-1α target, VEGFA is a HIF-2α target, AGXT is a normal kidney oxidative marker suppressed by VHL loss.

name: Warburg vs liver-like normal (KIRC)
biological_rationale: LDHA drives lactate production in tumor; ALB marks the normal-like liver-adjacent kidney metabolic state.

name: Glycolysis + hypoxia vs normal kidney
biological_rationale: SLC2A1 (GLUT1) and NDUFA4L2 are HIF targets driving glucose uptake and suppressing OXPHOS; AGXT marks normal proximal tubule.

name: Angiogenic ratio (KIRC)
biological_rationale: VEGFA upregulation vs ALB decline captures the angiogenic switch driven by VHL loss.

name: Single-gene anchor CA9 (KIRC)
biological_rationale: CA9 single-gene baseline — must be beaten by multi-gene laws to justify increased complexity.

name: Housekeeping null (KIRC negative control)
biological_rationale: Contrast between two housekeeping genes — NOT expected to separate tumor from normal. Included as deliberate ex-ante negative control so the demo can show the falsification gate killing a weak law alongside the rescued strong ones.

name: Random proliferation guess (KIRC negative control)
biological_rationale: MKI67 is a generic proliferation marker and RPL13A is a housekeeping reference. Neither is KIRC-specific; expected to fail the falsification gate.

name: Proliferation + glycolysis vs surfactant (LUAD)
biological_rationale: CDK1 and SLC2A1 mark KRAS/EGFR-driven proliferation and metabolic reprogramming; SFTPC marks normal alveolar type II cells.

name: Warburg vs normal lung (LUAD)
biological_rationale: LDHA marks the Warburg effect; NAPSA is expressed in normal type II pneumocytes and lost in high-grade LUAD.

name: EGFR-driven proliferation vs surfactant (LUAD)
biological_rationale: VEGFA + CDK1 mark angiogenic + proliferative EGFR/KRAS signals; SFTPB marks normal lung surfactant biology.
